records.ety: [F. dbcle, fr. dbcler to unbar, break loose; pref. d- (prob. = L. dis) + bcler to bolt, fr. L. baculum a stick.]

defs: A breaking or bursting forth; a violent rush or flood of waters which breaks down opposing barriers, and hurls forward and disperses blocks of stone and other dbris.
A sudden breaking up or breaking loose; a violent dispersion or disruption; impetuous rush; outburst.
a complete and ludicrous failure; a rout, as of an army; a great disaster; a fiasco.
